goal: Give Erik a scannable demo-day risk checklist for a G1 warehouse-picking sim with no time to retrain — surface sim-to-real gaps, lighting, and contact slippage risks.
thoughts: Erik is CTO, 15 min, no retrain. Respond with 3-5 prioritized bullets, not paragraphs. Real cardboard mu is 0.3-0.6 dry, much lower oily — if the sim uses the PhysX default, grasp behavior is a lie. Lighting variance from German fluorescents is a perception risk even for scripted pick. Do NOT give a single 'safe' friction number — qualify every number. Avoid any recommendation that takes >24h.
code:
# 1. Inventory the scene Erik has NOT touched in 2 weeks
scene_summary()

# 2. Contact + solver sanity — the cardboard grasp lives or dies here
check_physics_health()

# 3. Look up the physics material actually bound to the box/gripper contact pair
lookup_material(query="cardboard friction PhysX default vs measured values")

# 4. Ground lighting-DR guidance in docs, not vibes
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim fluorescent lighting variance demo perception sim-to-real checklist")

# Output structure (Slack-ready, 3-5 bullets):
# RED:    <e.g. mu_box_gripper = 0.8 default, likely 0.3-0.6 real -> grasp slip>
# YELLOW: <joint torque headroom on G1 shoulder in current demo trajectory>
# YELLOW: <lighting variance from overhead fluorescents — run one viz pass under HDRI>
# GREEN:  <scripted reset + recovery behavior if box drops>
# Monitor-live (no retrain): <live joint effort, live contact force peaks on fingertips>
failure_modes: Giving a single 'safe' friction number without qualifying measurement conditions
Recommending anything that needs retraining — blows the 24h budget
Long academic explanation — Erik closes the tab within 30 seconds
Missing the fluorescent-lighting risk for the German demo room
